channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Many Christian voters in the US see Trump as ‘chosen’ by God to lead
item.description: Many white evangelical Christians view Donald Trump as chosen by God to lead despite not seeing him as personally devout

item.title: Argentina players receive heroes’ welcome home despite World Cup defeat
item.description: Argentina’s players were welcomed home by fans lining the streets as the team travelled from the airport.

item.title: China and Philippines trade accusations after sailor injured at sea
item.description: Dispute erupts in South China Sea after incident near Second Thomas Shoal involving Chinese and Philippine vessels.

item.title: Lebanese return to ‘pilot zone’ villages they say Israel never occupied
item.description: Under a US-brokered plan, Israel is to hand over control of some areas it occupies in Lebanon to Lebanese government.

item.title: Spain’s World Cup heroes welcomed by millions in Madrid
item.description: More than half of Madrid’s population turned out to celebrate La Roja as the players returned to Spain

item.title: US military releases footage of tenth consecutive night of strikes on Iran
item.description: US Central Command (CENTCOM) released footage of its forces launching the latest round of strikes against Iran.

item.title: Who is driving diplomacy in the Horn of Africa and the Red Sea, and why?
item.description: A series of diplomatic consultations in Cairo underscores the Horn of Africa's growing strategic importance.

item.title: Guyana recovers 27 bodies after ferry disaster as crew comes under scrutiny
item.description: Authorities detain ferry crew members after drug tests reveal violations in Guyana's worst marine disaster in decades.

item.title: Why protests are growing in Ukraine over defence leadership and dismissals
item.description: Protests continue as anger grows over President Zelenskyy’s decision to fire the country’s defence minister.

item.title: Tate brothers vow to fight extradition as they appear at court in Miami
item.description: Controversial ‘manosphere’ influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared at a federal court hearing in Miami.

item.title: Trump imposes 50% US tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing discrimination
item.description: Tariffs apply to Canadian wine, hockey sticks, cement, and other products, sparking fears of escalating trade tensions.

item.title: US attacks Iran for 10th consecutive night
item.description: Washington vows revenge for US military casualties, while Iran claims attacks on US sites across the region.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran attacks Gulf states, says 2 tankers in Hormuz on fire
item.description: Explosions reported in Iran's Bandar Abbas, Qeshm Island, Chabahar and Konarak as US launches new wave of attacks.

item.title: MSF demands Israel release detained Palestinian healthcare workers
item.description: The NGO is 'outraged' by Israel's treatment of the health professionals and warns that 'silence has become complicity'.

item.title: Suspect charged in fatal attack on UK politician Ann Widdecombe
item.description: Police say a possible political motive is under investigation in the killing of the Reform UK figure.

item.title: Argentina, minus Messi, return home after World Cup final heartbreak
item.description: Thousands wait to greet the team, who return without captain Messi and a few other players after the World Cup.

item.title: What are the new British Prime Minister’s biggest challenges?
item.description: Britain has a new Prime Minister: Andy Burnham. The former Mayor of Manchester must now unite a divided nation.

item.title: Chaos in India as ‘Cockroach activists’ march on parliament
item.description: Chaos outside India's parliament as protesters tried to storm it; satirical Cockroach Janta Party gains traction.

item.title: War on Iran: War resumes 30 days after MoU
item.description: 30 days into the US-Iran MoU the worst fighting in months has erupted threatening to unravel the ceasefire.

item.title: Moroccan-born man dies while detained by police in Italy
item.description: A video showing the final moments of Moroccan-born Abderrahim Fakir as he was detained by police has sparked protests.

item.title: World Cup champions Spain return to royal welcome, parade through Madrid
item.description: Tens of thousands of fans greet La Roja as they travel down Madrid's historic avenues to Cibeles Square.

item.title: Albanian protesters pelt lawmakers’ cars over resort projects
item.description: Protesters threw eggs at lawmakers' vehicles outside Albania's parliament, demanding changes to laws.

item.title: FIFA investigating on-pitch brawl after Spain vs Argentina World Cup final
item.description: Argentina's Paredes was seen shoving Spanish players, which led to an on-field melee, while Spain's Yamal prayed calmly.

item.title: Trump rejects Mamdani’s threat to arrest Israel’s Netanyahu for war crimes
item.description: US president insists Netanyahu will not be arrested, despite ICC warrant.

item.title: King of Spain celebrates World Cup winners in Madrid
item.description: Spain’s World Cup-winning team received a royal reception from King Felipe VI in Madrid ahead of a victory parade.
